title: Goals, Context, Outputs, Boundaries, and Done Conditions · Prompt Lens: Done Conditions
summary: Write a production-ready prompt for a high-impact code and document change. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt done conditions increment in a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting.
Name material do-not-change, authorization, safety, and escalation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Done Conditions
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern

steps.title: Build Done Conditions evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-done-conditions-129gu7" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Done Conditions into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-done-conditions-129gu7
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Done Conditions model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Done Conditions applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting.
